finding: Two Codex captain instances can operate on the same task using the same actor id and claim surface, making active-work warnings look like self-continuation instead of a distinct concurrent session.
expectedBehavior: ATM should surface a distinct session or ticket identity for each captain instance and warn when another live session touches the same task, even if actor id and task id match.
evidenceOrRepro: During TASK-RFT-0037, ATM surfaced active work and branch queue signals, but the shared codex-task-rft-0037 actor/claim made the other Codex captain indistinguishable from the current continuation until commit-status showed a separate in-flight owner PID and the user flagged the collision.
followUp: Open a governance task to add session-key or ticket-level identity to broker status, next active-work summaries, branch commit queue diagnostics, and task claim warnings; include a regression where two sessions share actor/task but must be reported as distinct operators.
